text: Example Co's platform turns intake requests into a routed, auditable approval workflow that a manager reviews before anything is committed.
guardrail: Capability claim; state it builds a draft for review, not auto-commit. (legacy-shaped: no role tag — proves backward compatibility.)

text: Spend against budget updates live as requests are approved, so overruns surface before commitment rather than at month-end.
guardrail: Do not attach a cost-saving percentage.

resonance.note: Operator-noted: lands with finance buyers in multi-site accounts. Human-write-only — populated from the outcome ledger by a person, never auto-inferred.

pattern: We already have a tool for this, so we don't need this.
response: It sits in front of your existing tool, not on top of it: it captures the inputs and feeds them in, so the current system keeps running but stops being fed by spreadsheets. Position as complement, not replacement.
guardrail: Differentiation framing only; never disparage the prospect's named vendor.

text: Multi-site teams cannot see live cost per site until after month-end, so overspend is only visible in hindsight.
guardrail: A pain hypothesis to probe in discovery, not a stated fact about the specific prospect.

differentiation: Built-in automation that updates itself when the rules change — positioned as automation rather than a configure-it-yourself rules engine.
guardrail: State our differentiation only; do NOT assert what the competitor does or does not do — that is a claim about them, not us.
